We have many reasons for loving the Lord, but we are most deeply moved by his kindness when relieved from great distress. When a poor sinner is awakened to an understanding of their condition, and fears that they must soon perish under God’s just anger, then they experience trouble and sorrow. But let all such call upon the Lord to deliver their souls, and they will find him gracious and true to his promise. Neither ignorance nor guilt will prevent their salvation when they put their trust in the Lord. Let us all speak of God as we have found him; and have we ever found him otherwise than just and good? It is because of his mercies that we are not destroyed. Let those who labor and are burdened come to him, so that they may find rest for their souls; and if at any time they are drawn away from their rest, let them quickly return, remembering how generously the Lord has treated them. We should feel obligated to walk as in his presence. It is a great mercy to be kept from being overwhelmed with excessive sorrow. It is a great mercy for God to hold us by the right hand, so that we are not overcome and defeated by temptation. But when we enter into heavenly rest, deliverance from sin and sorrow will be complete; we will behold the glory of the Lord, and walk in his presence with delight we cannot now imagine.

When troubled, it is best for us to remain silent, because we are prone to speak without thinking. Yet there may be true faith where there are workings of unbelief; but then faith will prevail; and being humbled for our distrust of God’s word, we will experience his faithfulness to it. What can the forgiven sinner, or those who have been delivered from trouble or distress, give back to the Lord for his benefits? We cannot in any way profit him. Our best is unworthy of his acceptance; yet we ought to devote ourselves and all that we have to his service. I will take the cup of salvation; I will offer the drink-offerings appointed by the law, as a token of thankfulness to God, and rejoice in God’s goodness to me. I will receive the cup of affliction; that cup, that bitter cup, which is sanctified for the saints, so that it is a cup of salvation for them; it is a means of spiritual health. The cup of consolation; I will receive the benefits God bestows upon me, as from his hand, and taste his love in them, as part not only of my inheritance in the other world, but also of my experience in this one. Let others serve whatever masters they choose; truly, I am your servant. People come to be servants in two ways: by birth. Lord, I was born in your house; I am the son of your handmaid, and therefore your. It is a great mercy to be children of godly parents. By redemption. Lord, you have loosed my bonds; you have discharged me from them, so I am your servant. The bonds you have loosed will tie me even closer to you. Doing good is a sacrifice that pleases God; and this must accompany giving thanks in his name. Why should we offer to the Lord something that costs us nothing? The psalmist will pay his vows now; he will not delay payment: publicly, not to boast, but to show that he is not ashamed of serving God, and to invite others to join him. These are true saints of God, in whose lives and deaths he will be glorified.
